summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that when OAuth2Required is false (controlled by NRF), UDM accepts unauthenticated requests to all endpoints without token validation.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that unauthenticated HTTP requests to UDM endpoints succeed when OAuth2 is disabled via NRF configuration.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh started mock NRF returning oauth2=false, built UDM service, and unauthenticated requests to /nudm-sdm/v2/test/am-data succeeded with HTTP 200.
verification_method: The verify.sh script sends unauthenticated requests and checks for HTTP 200 response, confirming complete authentication bypass.
evidence_produced_by_run: Mock NRF returned customInfo with oauth2=false
Unauthenticated GET request to /nudm-sdm/v2/test/am-data returned HTTP 200
No OAuth2 token required for any endpoint access

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the free5gc/udm repository from GitHub, checks out v3.2.10 tag, and builds the UDM service.
